evals.prompt:
I just installed kgateway v2.3.1 on my cluster and created a Gateway and HTTPRoute, but the route shows 'Accepted: False'. Here's my HTTPRoute yaml:

apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: my-app
  namespace: app-ns
spec:
  parentRefs:
  - name: http
    namespace: kgateway-system
  rules:
  - backendRefs:
    - name: my-service
      port: 8080

What could be wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnose the Accepted: False condition — likely allowedRoutes namespaces config or missing ReferenceGrant. Provide fix with kubectl commands and YAML

assertions.name: identifies-cross-namespace-as-root-cause
assertions.description: Response identifies that the HTTPRoute is in a different namespace (app-ns) than the Gateway (kgateway-system) as the likely root cause

assertions.name: mentions-allowedRoutes-or-namespace-config
assertions.description: Response mentions allowedRoutes on the Gateway listener OR explains namespace-scoped route filtering

assertions.name: provides-kubectl-describe-command
assertions.description: Response includes a kubectl describe httproute or kubectl describe gateway command to inspect conditions

assertions.name: provides-fix-yaml-or-command
assertions.description: Response includes either a YAML fix (allowedRoutes: namespaces: from: All) or a ReferenceGrant example to resolve the issue

evals.prompt: How do I set up JWT authentication on my kgateway HTTPRoute? I'm using Auth0 and want to require a valid JWT for all requests to /api/* but allow /api/public without auth.
evals.expected_output: TrafficPolicy YAML with JWT provider pointing to Auth0 JWKS, plus how to exempt /api/public with a second route rule. Include issuer/audience config.

assertions.name: shows-TrafficPolicy-with-jwt
assertions.description: Response includes a TrafficPolicy YAML with spec.policy.jwt.providers

assertions.name: includes-issuer-and-audiences
assertions.description: Response includes issuer and audiences fields in the JWT provider config

assertions.name: shows-auth0-jwks-url
assertions.description: Response includes the Auth0 JWKS URL pattern (e.g., https://<tenant>.auth0.com/.well-known/jwks.json)

assertions.name: addresses-public-route-exclusion
assertions.description: Response explains how to exempt /api/public from JWT auth (e.g., separate HTTPRoute rule, ordering, or no-policy route)

evals.prompt: We're doing a canary deploy and want to send 10% of traffic to our new version v2 while keeping 90% on v1. How do I configure this in kgateway?
evals.expected_output: HTTPRoute with weighted backendRefs (weight 90/10), explanation of relative weights, mention of Argo Rollouts integration for automated progression

assertions.name: shows-weighted-backendRefs
assertions.description: Response includes an HTTPRoute with backendRefs containing weight fields

assertions.name: correct-weight-ratio
assertions.description: Response uses weight: 90 and weight: 10 (or equivalent 9/1 ratio) for v1 and v2

assertions.name: explains-weight-semantics
assertions.description: Response explains that weights are relative (e.g., they don't need to sum to 100)

assertions.name: mentions-argo-rollouts-or-progression
assertions.description: Response mentions Argo Rollouts integration or progressive delivery as a next step

evals.prompt: I'm upgrading from kgateway v2.2 to v2.3. What do I need to know and watch out for?
evals.expected_output: List v2.3.0 breaking changes: classic transformation removed, CORS wildcard syntax change, XListenerSet→ListenerSet migration, Istio ServiceEntry KGW_ENABLE_ISTIO_INTEGRATION flag. Include upgrade Helm commands.

assertions.name: mentions-classic-transformation-removed
assertions.description: Response mentions that the classic transformation filter was removed and only Rustformation is supported in v2.3

assertions.name: mentions-cors-wildcard-change
assertions.description: Response mentions the CORS wildcard origin syntax change (non-spec patterns rejected)

assertions.name: mentions-xlistenerset-migration
assertions.description: Response mentions the XListenerSet to ListenerSet migration (kind and apiVersion change)

assertions.name: mentions-istio-integration-flag
assertions.description: Response mentions the KGW_ENABLE_ISTIO_INTEGRATION env var / flag for Istio ServiceEntry watching

assertions.name: provides-helm-upgrade-commands
assertions.description: Response includes the Helm upgrade commands for kgateway-crds and kgateway charts

evals.prompt: I need to debug why requests to my service are getting 503s. The HTTPRoute looks correct and the pods are running. How do I investigate using kgateway's debugging tools?
evals.expected_output: Systematic debug steps: kubectl describe httproute, port-forward to admin interfaces (9095 and 19000), config_dump, cluster stats, log level increase commands

assertions.name: mentions-control-plane-admin-port-9095
assertions.description: Response mentions port 9095 for the kgateway control plane admin interface

assertions.name: mentions-proxy-admin-port-19000
assertions.description: Response mentions port 19000 for the Envoy gateway proxy admin interface

assertions.name: mentions-config-dump-or-clusters
assertions.description: Response mentions config_dump, clusters, or another Envoy admin endpoint to inspect configuration

assertions.name: provides-systematic-checklist
assertions.description: Response provides a structured/ordered set of diagnostic steps rather than a single command

assertions.name: includes-log-inspection
assertions.description: Response includes kubectl logs command for either the control plane or proxy deployment
